# Phase 9.1.3 Fail-Closed Survivability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Document how containment failure, partial governance degradation, authority confusion, and hidden activation attempts are reviewed while preserving fail-closed behavior.

## Scope

- Containment failure doctrine.
- Fail-closed evidence review.
- Non-executable containment degradation simulations.
- Escalation confusion controls.
- Partial failure survivability.
- Authority-boundary failure analysis.
- Hidden activation skepticism.
- Governance fallback survivability.
- Runtime ambiguity containment.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No containment-review system creates runtime authority.
- No survivability simulation bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No ambiguity-management layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No fallback-governance layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No fail-closed review weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows meta-consistency and unknown-risk doctrine.
- Safe to include now: tabletop-only scenario review and evidence philosophy.
- Future upgrade: standardize containment evidence packets and blocker closure records.
- Do not include: executable simulations, provider tests, infrastructure changes, or runtime pathways.
